summary: April 23 moves the public decision trail forward to Loamspike Burrower, the first Rootline Defense enemy concept built around a reusable burrow behavior that bypasses the front defender line and reopens depth-defense play.

candidates.title: Loamspike Burrower — Rootline Defense's First Enemy That Tunnels Under Your Wall
candidates.summary: A ground enemy with a reusable burrow behavior that telegraphs, dives, resurfaces behind the frontmost defender, and forces the player to stop treating Amber Wall as a total answer.

keep: The concept adds a reusable enemy contract rather than a one-off scripted exception.
mustChange: Keep the tutorial explanation short and let the burrow telegraph do most of the teaching.
risks: If the telegraph is unclear, players will read the burrow as unfair rather than strategic.

keep: Burrow pressure breaks the front-stack Amber Wall default without devaluing Amber Wall itself.
mustChange: Validator and browser replay coverage need to stay explicit because burrow adds another authority-shifted enemy class.
risks: If the board is tuned too tightly around one answer, the day becomes a recipe rather than a lesson.

keep: The state-machine framing is clear: telegraph, underground invulnerability, surface, then resume normal walker rules.
mustChange: Make the Board Scout callout and in-play surface effect legible enough that the new verb reads before the first failure.
risks: If underpass timing is too long, the new enemy can feel like dead air instead of pressure.

keep: The day compounds directly on Amber Wall and Cottonburr Mortar instead of resetting the game's direction.
mustChange: Burrow needs strong validator and replay coverage because it deliberately bypasses the front line.
risks: The new enemy is only compelling if the surfacing moment is both visible and strategically meaningful.

candidates.title: Board Proof — Browser-Verified Challenge Replay
candidates.summary: An opt-in replay artifact that proves a real winning line for the current challenge and teaches the roster through browser-verified play instead of static explanation alone.

candidates.title: Seed Tray Draft — Pre-Run Five-Plant Loadout Screen
candidates.summary: A pre-game drafting surface that makes the growing Rootline Defense roster a strategic choice by forcing the player to pick a constrained loadout before starting the daily board.

winner.title: Loamspike Burrower — Rootline Defense's First Enemy That Tunnels Under Your Wall
winner.summary: A ground enemy with a reusable burrow behavior that telegraphs, dives, resurfaces behind the frontmost defender, and forces the player to stop treating Amber Wall as a total answer.

rationale: Loamspike Burrower won because it compounds directly on the current Rootline Defense roster, adds a reusable enemy behavior instead of another one-off exception, and creates a clear, legible pressure pattern that the homepage, day-detail archive, and judge scoreboard can surface immediately.

feedbackInfluence.summary: The recent run of Rootline Defense days needed a pressure source that specifically punishes front-only defense instead of asking for more raw damage.
The public artifact trail needed to advance to the next dated day so the homepage, recent archive, and decision links stop stalling on the April 22 republish entry.
